meta.name: Multi-Timeframe Strategy Validation
meta.description: Validates whether V5 strategy (designed for 1H) generalizes to 15m and 30m timeframes. If a shorter timeframe passes validation, a parallel forward test can be launched for faster data accumulation — without touching the existing 1H forward test.

motivation.problem: 1H forward test generates ~1 trade per 5 days per coin. Meaningful results (30+ trades) require 4-6 weeks. This is structurally slow and cannot be accelerated without changing timeframe.
motivation.question: Does V5 strategy work on shorter timeframes (15m, 30m)? If yes, a parallel forward test on 15m would generate 4x more data points and reach statistical significance in 1-2 weeks instead of 4-6.
motivation.risk: V5 was optimized on 1H. Shorter timeframes have different ATR magnitudes, more noise in Market Structure signals, noisier FVG fills, and higher relative commission impact. The strategy may fail badly.
motivation.decision_framework: Run full validation suite on 15m and 30m. If PASS (3/4+ checks), launch parallel forward test. If FAIL, do NOT launch — wait for 1H results.

existing_files.validation_suite: validate_strategy.py — 4-test robustness suite (OOS, Monte Carlo, Sensitivity, Multi-Asset)

steps.title: Download 15m and 30m historical data
steps.expected: 6 CSV files in data/ directory, each with 50,000-100,000 candles
notes: Start from 2024-01-01 (not 2023) — shorter timeframes generate more bars, 1 year is sufficient
PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 may be needed on Windows for console output

spec.description: Runs the same 4 validation tests from validate_strategy.py on 15m and 30m data. Uses identical V5 WINNER_PARAMS — no parameter tuning for shorter timeframes.

structure.imports: Same as validate_strategy.py (numpy, pandas, Backtester, walk_forward_test, SizingMethod)
structure.WINNER_PARAMS: Exact copy of V5 params from validate_strategy.py — DO NOT MODIFY for shorter timeframes. The whole point is testing if 1H params generalize as-is.

tests_per_timeframe.name: Out-of-Sample (80/20 split)
tests_per_timeframe.logic: Same as validate_strategy.py test_out_of_sample(). Load largest CSV for symbol+interval, 80% train / 20% test, check OOS profitable.

tests_per_timeframe.name: Monte Carlo (10,000 shuffles)
tests_per_timeframe.logic: Same as validate_strategy.py test_monte_carlo(). Run strategy on BTC data for the interval, shuffle PnLs 10,000 times.

tests_per_timeframe.name: Sensitivity Analysis
tests_per_timeframe.logic: Same as validate_strategy.py test_sensitivity(). Vary SL mult (1.0-3.5), TP1 mult (1.0-3.0), Risk pct (0.01-0.03), Confluence (0.25-0.75).

tests_per_timeframe.name: Multi-Asset Generalization
tests_per_timeframe.logic: Same as validate_strategy.py test_multi_asset(). Run on BTC, ETH, SOL for this interval. Include 5-fold walk-forward per asset.

per_timeframe_summary: Run all 4 tests, print results exactly like validate_strategy.py
Print checklist: 5 checks (OOS, MC loss, MC DD, multi-asset, SL sensitivity)

logic: If 15m scores 4+/5 AND Sharpe > 2.0 AND PF > 2.0: 'RECOMMENDED — launch 15m parallel forward test'
If 15m scores 3/5: 'MARGINAL — review metrics before deciding'
If 15m scores <3/5: 'NOT RECOMMENDED — stay with 1H only'

important_notes: DO NOT modify WINNER_PARAMS for shorter timeframes. If V5 params don't work on 15m, that's the answer.
The load_data() function selects the LARGEST CSV for a given symbol+interval pattern. Ensure fresh data was downloaded in step 1.
Print comparison with 1H results. 1H reference values: BTC +1093%, Sharpe 5.43, DD 8.57%, WR 85%, PF 12.75, 235 trades.
Use PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 on Windows to handle Unicode box-drawing characters.

steps.title: Run the multi-timeframe validation
steps.expected_output: Full validation results for 15m and 30m, comparison table, final recommendation

steps.title: Report results back
instructions: Copy the FULL terminal output from step 3
Paste it in the next Claude session
Include the final comparison table and recommendation
Claude will analyze the results and decide whether to launch a parallel forward test

details: Add 5 new rows to live_portfolios for 15m (BTCUSDT_15m, etc.)
Modify cron to process both 1H and 15m intervals
Dashboard: add interval toggle to switch between 1H and 15m views
Telegram: include interval tag in notifications

if_15m_fails_30m_passes.details: Same as above but with 30m interval. 2x faster than 1H, not as fast as 15m but more reliable.

if_both_fail.details: The strategy is 1H-specific. Wait for 1H forward test results (4-6 weeks). Use the time for signal attribution analysis or other research.

constraints: DO NOT modify V5 WINNER_PARAMS for shorter timeframes — test them AS-IS
DO NOT touch the existing 1H forward test, cron route, or any production code
DO NOT deploy anything — this is a local research task only
Data download uses public Binance API, no API key needed
validate_timeframes.py is a standalone script, does not import from Next.js code
All results are printed to stdout — no database writes, no file outputs
If Binance rate-limits, add sleep(0.5) between requests (data_fetcher.py already handles this)
